notes: Released Dec 15 1999 (RTM); Feb 17 2000 (GA); superseded by XP 2001 / Server 2003
Predecessor to NT 5.0; Service Pack 4 delivered many robustness fixes in Oct 1998
New directory service in NT 5.0; Aberdeen warned mid/large orgs would need major effort to deploy
Enterprise Edition feature; required careful testing; succeeded by Windows Server Failover Clustering
Client-data mirroring technology in NT 5.0; absorbed into Group Policy and other management tools
Added to NT 5.0 for enhanced security; Aberdeen flagged interoperability concerns with non-Microsoft environments
Added to NT 5.0; Aberdeen noted Microsoft had not resolved heterogeneous interoperability
NT 5.0 security feature; concept remains critical in modern identity management
Part of ZAW initiative; evolved into System Center Configuration Manager
Microsoft planned 64-bit NT 5.0 for IA-64; Itanium released 2001 but never achieved mass market; discontinued 2021
Microsoft initiative to reduce Windows admin burden; succeeded by Group Policy and later Intune
New admin framework in NT 5.0; still in use in Windows Server today
